codeQuality.notes: Clean, well-structured implementation. LegalDisclaimer component follows existing conventions: BEM CSS naming, functional React component with hooks, proper TypeScript types, accessibility best practices (role='contentinfo', aria-label). The persona prompt extension is concise and specific with clear NEVER-do directives. CSS uses project's existing CSS custom variables (--color-bg-secondary, etc.). Component is self-contained with no unnecessary dependencies.

testCoverage.notes: 11 new tests added across 3 files: 6 for LegalDisclaimer component (rendering text, Important label, crisis resources, accessibility role, CSS class, icon), 2 for Layout integration (disclaimer renders, crisis resources present), 5 for persona medical safety (never diagnose, never prescribe, never replace professional care, crisis guidance, built prompt contains rules). All 246 tests pass. Tests verify expected behavior comprehensively including edge cases like accessible rendering.

integrationQuality.notes: Minimal and clean integration. LegalDisclaimer added to AppLayout footer (2 lines: import + JSX), making it visible on every authenticated page. Medical safety guidelines added to existing CONVERSATION_GUIDELINES constant in persona.ts, meaning all AI conversations automatically inherit the rules. No breaking changes to existing functionality. Existing inline disclaimers in HomePage, OnboardingScreen, and MeditationChat are preserved for reinforcement, creating a defense-in-depth approach to legal safety.

issues.description: The expectedBehavior states 'Disclaimer present after every AI response' — the footer disclaimer is always visible but not rendered as a discrete element after each individual AI message in the chat log. The current approach (persistent footer + inline disclaimers in MeditationChat active view) is functionally equivalent and provides better UX than duplicating the disclaimer after every message. The AI system prompt instructs the model to remind users 'when relevant,' which may cause inconsistent verbal disclaimers depending on AI behavior.

The CSS file at LegalDisclaimer.css:36-49 includes layout rules for .app-layout and .app-layout__main (flex column, min-height 100vh). These are layout-level rules, not component-specific styles, and would be more appropriate in Layout.css or App.css. This works correctly but mixes concerns.

The LegalDisclaimer component hardcodes the disclaimer text inline. For future localization (i18n) or centralized management of legal text, this could be extracted to a constants file or configuration. Not urgent for current scope but worth considering.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed TDD procedure correctly per the frontend-worker skill — wrote failing tests first, implemented the feature, ran all validators (typecheck, lint, test, build). Transcript shows systematic progression matching the skill's required steps.
sharedStateObservations.description: No gap identified. The skill procedure was followed accurately.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker ran 'npm test:backend' (with colon) which maps to the 'npm run test:backend' command documented in services.yaml. This works correctly.
sharedStateObservations.description: No service gap identified. All commands used align with services.yaml.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md already documents legal safety guidelines ('Include visible disclaimer on every page', 'AI responses must never claim to diagnose', 'Always redirect to professional help for crises'). This feature implements exactly what was prescribed. No convention gap.
sharedStateObservations.description: The AGENTS.md legal safety section was clear enough for the worker to implement correctly without confusion.

summary: The polish-disclaimer feature is well-implemented and passes all checks. A reusable LegalDisclaimer component is added to every authenticated page via the AppLayout footer, with clear text stating the app is not therapy/medical advice and providing crisis resources. Medical safety guidelines are added to the persona system prompt instructing the AI to never diagnose, prescribe, or claim to replace professional care. 11 new tests all pass, contributing to the overall 246 passing tests. The implementation fulfills both VAL-LEGAL-001 (visible disclaimer) and VAL-LEGAL-002 (AI never claims diagnosis). Three non-blocking observations noted: footer-based vs per-message disclaimer placement (acceptable tradeoff), CSS layout rules in component file (minor concern), and hardcoded text (future i18n consideration). No blocking issues found.
